headline: Aquaculture Industry Targets RM8B But Pollution Limits Growth
context: The Fisheries Department reported aquaculture output of RM5.4 billion in 2025, short of the RM8 billion National Aquaculture Plan target, as coastal pollution and disease outbreaks destroyed 22% of shrimp farm production in Perak and Johor.

cards.big: RM5.4 billion aquaculture output — 32% below target as polluted waters kill 22% of shrimp farm harvests
cards.sub: Malaysia imports RM6.2 billion in seafood while domestic aquaculture potential goes unrealised

cards.big: Shrimp mortality from Early Mortality Syndrome wiped out RM890 million in production across 1,400 farms in 2025
cards.sub: Disease outbreaks linked to industrial effluent contamination of coastal aquaculture zones in Perak's Manjung district

cards.big: 12,000 aquaculture workers — 78% from B40 families — lost income averaging RM1,800 monthly from farm closures
cards.sub: Small-scale fish farmers receive no crop insurance equivalent — one disease outbreak means total financial loss

cards.big: DOE's water quality monitoring covers only 38% of designated aquaculture zones — polluters upstream face minimal enforcement
cards.sub: Fisheries Department issues aquaculture licences without coordinating with DOE on water quality data for the same zones

cards.big: You can't grow fish in polluted water — Malaysia's aquaculture ambitions die upstream where factories dump waste unmonitored
Should aquaculture zones receive legally protected water quality standards with automatic polluter shutdown mechanisms?
